instruction: Using Terminal and sqlite3, in ~/db/{DB} add a row to the tasks table with title {TARGET}. Do not change any existing row.
source: cove-original (design 047 §5 before/after integrity, §7 WAL checkpoint); fully VM-free self-checkable — the AndroidWorld validate_rows_addition_integrity collateral-damage check over a Terminal-driven sqlite db

args: rm -rf "$HOME/db"; mkdir -p "$HOME/db" "$HOME/.guibench"; sqlite3 "$HOME/db/{DB}" 'CREATE TABLE tasks(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT);'; sqlite3 "$HOME/db/{DB}" "INSERT INTO tasks(id,title) VALUES(1,'Standup'),(2,'Deploy');"; sqlite3 "$HOME/db/{DB}" "SELECT id||'|'||title FROM tasks ORDER BY id" > "$HOME/.guibench/{DB}.before"

result.query: SELECT id||'|'||title FROM tasks ORDER BY id;
